records.morph: [imp. Wiredrew (?); p. p. Wiredrawn (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Wiredrawing.]

defs: To form (a piece of metal) into wire, by drawing it through a hole in a plate of steel.
Hence, to draw by art or violence.
quotes: My sense has been wiredrawn into blasphemy.

defs: Hence, also, to draw or spin out to great length and tenuity; as, to wiredraw an argument.
quotes: Such twisting, such wiredrawing, was never seen in a court of justice.

defs: To pass, or to draw off, (as steam) through narrow ports, or the like, thus reducing its pressure or force by friction.
